question: Use the nemo-mbridge-recipe-recommender skill. Recommend recipes for these exact Megatron Bridge cases: Qwen3 30B-A3B SFT on 16 GPUs, Qwen3 235B-A22B PEFT on 16 GPUs, Llama3 8B 128K pretrain, and first-time Bridge tryout. Include the entry point, datasets, library-vs-benchmark recipe distinction, key adjustment rules, and change-control classification.
ground_truth: The answer should use the recipe recommender skill. It should recommend qwen3_30b_a3b_sft_config with the tulu3 dataset for Qwen3 30B-A3B SFT on 16 GPUs, qwen3_235b_a22b_peft_config with a finetuning dataset such as squad or tulu3 for Qwen3 235B-A22B PEFT on 16 GPUs, llama3_8b_128k_pretrain_config with the mock dataset for Llama3 8B 128K pretrain, and llama3_8b_pretrain_config with the mock dataset as the first-time Bridge tryout. It should name scripts/training/run_recipe.py with uv run python -m torch.distributed.run for library recipes and distinguish benchmark recipes under src/megatron/bridge/perf_recipes from production training recipes. It should explain that exact benchmark recipes can run through scripts/training/train.sh, use their canonical dataset and total GPU count, and may use scripts/performance/setup_experiment.py for compatibility workflows. It should include adjustment rules: TP must divide num_key_value_heads, TP should stay within a node unless using NVL72-style interconnect, SP should be true whenever TP>1, CP needs cp_comm_type and long-context variants or overrides, dense and expert parallel meshes must both divide the world size, and micro_batch_size should be reduced first on OOM. It should classify proposed overrides as convergence changes, semantics-preserving execution changes, or benchmark-only shortcuts.
expected_behavior: Read the nemo-mbridge-recipe-recommender skill before answering.
Identify the task as recipe selection or customization.
Recommend the exact Qwen3, Llama3, and first-time recipes requested.
Name scripts/training/run_recipe.py and valid named dataset presets such as mock, squad, and tulu3.
Include recipe resizing rules for TP, SP, CP, DP, and micro batch size.
Distinguish library recipes from benchmark recipes and name the benchmark launcher paths.
Distinguish convergence-contract changes from semantics-preserving execution changes and benchmark-only shortcuts.
